LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROBERT MCCARTHY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. MANY OF THE ISSUES RAISED BY DEFENDANT INVOLVE QUESTIONS OF FACT AND CANNOT BE RESOLVED BY DEMURRER, AN EXAMPLE THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT ISSUE. EXHIBIT A TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT SETS FORTH FACTS ALLEGING CONSIDERATION AND THE AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES ALLEGE AN ORAL CONTRACT. PROBATE CODE SECTION 19100, IN EFFECT AT THE TIME, HAD A THIRTY DAY TIME LIMITATION AFTER ACTUAL NOTICE. THE COMPLAINT USES THE PHRASE "ON OR ABOUT' AND THAT PHRASE HAS BEEN HELD TO BE TOO AMBIGUIOUS TO SUSTAIN A DEMURRER BASED ON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS: LIPTAK V DIANE APARTMENTS, INC. (1980) 109 CAL APP 3D 762,775 AND CHILDS V STATE (1983) 144 CAL APP 3D 155. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MCCARTHY AS TRUSTEE OF THE HENRY SHWEID AND MUNZIKA SHWEID INTERVIVOS REVOCABLE TRUST DECLARATION OF 1992'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OF PLAINTIFF CHILDREN DAY SCHOOL'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (C.C.P. SECTION 437C). RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. THE PROBATE CODE DOES NOT NECESSARILY REQUIRE WRITTEN ACTUAL NOTICE TO THE CREDITOR IS THE ONLY FORM OF NOTICE THAT MAY BE GIVEN. THE PURPOSE OF NOTICE IS TO PROTECT THE PARTIES. WHERE THE TRUSTEE AND CREDITOR AGREE TO ORAL NOTICE, THERE IS NO REASON WHY THAT IS INSUFFICIENT. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE TRUSTEE'S ATTORNEY PROVIDED ORAL NOTICE ON 3/17/03 AND THAT PLAINTIFF, IN TURN, FILED A TIMELY CLAIM ON 4/11/03. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROBERT J. MCCARTHY AS TRUSTEE OF THE HENRY SHWEID AND MUNZIKA SHWEID INTERVIVOS REVOCABLE TRUST DECLARATION OF 1992'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OF PLAINTIFF CHILDREN DAY SCHOOL'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (C.C.P. SECTION 437c).
APPEARANCES: Philip F. Atkins-Pattenson, Esq. of Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton LLP 774-2933 for pltf & opposing party; Alexander Bannon, Esq. of McInerney & Dillon, P.C. 510/465-7100 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. THE PROBATE CODE DOES NOT NECESSARILY REQUIRE WRITTEN ACTUAL NOTICE TO THE CREDITOR IS THE ONLY FORM OF NOTICE THAT MAY BE GIVEN. THE PURPOSE OF NOTICE IS TO PROTECT THE PARTIES. WHERE THE TRUSTEE AND CREDITOR AGREE TO ORAL NOTICE, THERE IS NO REASON WHY THAT IS INSUFFICIENT. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE TRUSTEE'S ATTORNEY PROVIDED ORAL NOTICE ON 3/17/03 AND THAT PLAINTIFF, IN TURN, FILED A TIMELY CLAIM ON 4/11/03. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROBERT MCCARTHY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Philip Atkins-Pattenson of Sheppard Mullin (774-2933) for plaintiff and responding party
LeCarie Whitfield of McInerney & Dillon (510-465-7100) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: overruled, 10 days to answer. The record reflects that the demurrer was not timely filed. In addition, "In order for the bar of the statute of limitations to be raised by demurrer, the defect must clearly and affirmatively appear on the face of the complaint; it is not enough that the complaint shows that the action may be barred." Marshall v. Gibson Dunnis Crutcher (1995) 37 Cal App 4th 1397, 1403. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. 302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROBERT MCCARTHY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. MANY OF THE ISSUES RAISED BY DEFENDANT INVOLVE QUESTIONS OF FACT AND CANNOT BE RESOLVED BY DEMURRER, AN EXAMPLE THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT ISSUE. EXHIBIT A TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT SETS FORTH FACTS ALLEGING CONSIDERATION AND THE AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES ALLEGE AN ORAL CONTRACT. PROBATE CODE SECTION 19100, IN EFFECT AT THE TIME, HAD A THIRTY DAY TIME LIMITATION AFTER ACTUAL NOTICE. THE COMPLAINT USES THE PHRASE "ON OR ABOUT' AND THAT PHRASE HAS BEEN HELD TO BE TOO AMBIGUIOUS TO SUSTAIN A DEMURRER BASED ON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS: LIPTAK V DIANE APARTMENTS, INC. (1980) 109 CAL APP 3D 762,775 AND CHILDS V STATE (1983) 144 CAL APP 3D 155. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
